back_translation: And Ahithophel said to Absalom, 'Let me please choose twelve thousand men, and I will rise and pursue after David this night.
And I will come upon him while he is weary and his hands are slack, and I will throw him into a panic, and all the people who are with him will flee, and I will strike down the king alone.
And I will bring all the people back to you — as the returning of the whole — the man whom you seek. All the people will be at peace.'
And the matter was right in the eyes of Absalom and in the eyes of all the elders of Israel.
And Absalom said, 'Please call also for Hushai the Archite, and let us hear what he will say — he too.'
And Hushai came to Absalom, and Absalom said to him, saying, 'According to this word Ahithophel has spoken. Shall we do his word? If not, you speak.'
And Hushai said to Absalom, 'The counsel that Ahithophel has given at this time is not good.'

And Hushai said, 'You know your father and his men, that they are mighty warriors, and their soul is bitter, like a bear bereaved of cubs in the field; and your father is a man of war and will not lodge with the people.

Behold, now he is hiding in one of the caves or in one of the places, and it will be — when some among them fall in the beginning, the hearer will hear and say, There has been a slaughter among the people who are after Absalom.
And even the son of valor, whose heart is like the heart of a lion — he will melt, he will melt; for all Israel knows that your father is mighty, and sons of valor are with him.
For I have counseled: let all Israel be gathered — gathered to you, from Dan to Beersheba, like the sand that is on the sea for multitude, and your own face going into the battle.

And we will come upon him in one of the places where he is found there, and we will descend upon him as the dew falls upon the ground; and there will not remain of him or of all the men who are with him even one.
And if he gathers into a city, all Israel will bring to that city ropes, and we will drag it down to the wadi, until not even a pebble can be found there.'

And Absalom and all the men of Israel said, 'The counsel of Hushai the Archite is better than the counsel of Ahithophel.' And the LORD had commanded to thwart the good counsel of Ahithophel, in order that the LORD might bring the calamity upon Absalom.
And Hushai said to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, 'Thus and thus Ahithophel counseled Absalom and the elders of Israel, and thus and thus I myself have counseled.
And now, send quickly and tell David, saying, Do not lodge the night at the fords of the wilderness, but cross — cross over — lest the king be swallowed, and all the people who are with him.'
And Jonathan and Ahimaaz were standing at En-rogel, and the maidservant would go and tell them, and they would go and tell King David — for they could not be seen entering the city.
But a young man saw them and told Absalom, so the two of them went quickly and came to the house of a man in Bahurim, and he had a well in his courtyard, and they went down there.
And the woman took the covering and spread it over the face of the well, and scattered upon it the grain, and nothing was known.

And the servants of Absalom came to the woman at the house and said, 'Where are Ahimaaz and Jonathan?' And the woman said to them, 'They have crossed the brook of water.' And they searched and did not find, and they returned to Jerusalem.

And it happened, after their going, that they came up from the well and went and told King David, and they said to David, 'Arise and cross over the water quickly, for thus has Ahithophel counseled against you.'
And David arose, and all the people who were with him, and they crossed the Jordan; by the light of morning not even one was missing who had not crossed the Jordan.

And when Ahithophel saw that his counsel was not done, he saddled the donkey and arose and went to his house, to his city; and he commanded concerning his house and hanged himself, and died, and was buried in the tomb of his father.
And David came to Mahanaim, and Absalom crossed the Jordan — he and all the men of Israel with him.

And Absalom set Amasa over the army in place of Joab; and Amasa was the son of a man, and his name was Ithra the Israelite, who had gone in to Abigail, daughter of Nahash, sister of Zeruiah the mother of Joab.
And Israel and Absalom camped in the land of Gilead.
And it happened, when David came to Mahanaim, that Shobi son of Nahash from Rabbah of the sons of Ammon, and Machir son of Ammiel from Lo-debar, and Barzillai the Gileadite from Rogelim —
brought beds and basins and earthen vessels, and wheat and barley and flour and roasted grain, and beans and lentils and roasted grain,

and honey and curds and sheep and cheese of the herd — they presented to David and to the people who were with him to eat, for they said, 'The people are hungry and weary and thirsty in the wilderness.'
